title.en: MACHINES OR ENGINES FOR LIQUIDS (positive-displacement engines for liquid F03C; machines for liquids and gases F01; positive-displacement machines for liquids F04, rotary fluid gearing of the hydrokinetic type F16H41/00)
Engines of impulse type, i.e. turbines with jets of high-velocity liquid impinging on blades or like rotors, e.g. Pelton wheels; Parts or details peculiar thereto
Buckets; Bucket-carrying rotors
Nozzles (in general B05B); Nozzle-carrying members
Machines or engines of reaction type; Parts or details peculiar thereto
with radial flow at high-pressure side and axial flow at low-pressure side of rotors, e.g. Francis turbines {(rotors per se F03B3/125)}
with substantially axial flow throughout rotors, e.g. propeller turbines {(rotors per se F03B3/126)}
with pressure-velocity transformation exclusively in rotors
characterised by having means for functioning alternatively as pumps or turbines {(starting F03B15/005)}
Blades; Blade-carrying rotors
Stators
Machines or engines characterised by non-bladed rotors, e.g. serrated, using friction
Water wheels {(of swinging flap type F03B17/06)}
{with buckets receiving the liquid}
{of the endless-chain type}
Endless-chain machines or engines
{with buckets receiving the liquid}
Parts or details not provided for in, or of interest apart from, the preceding groups (controlling F03B15/00), {e.g. wear-protection couplings, between turbine and generator}
{Injecting air or other fluid (F03D80/40, F03B11/04, F03B15/00 take precedence)}
{Valve arrangements (F03B3/10 takes precedence; adjustable wicket gates F03B3/183; valves in general F16K)}
{Sealing arrangements (F03B3/14, F03B3/183, F03B13/083 takes precedence; sealings in general F16J)}
{Measuring or testing arrangements (in general G01)}
Casings {(spiral or volute casings F03B3/186)}
for diminishing cavitation or vibration, e.g. balancing
Bearing arrangements
for removing foreign matter, e.g. mud

Adaptations of machines or engines for special use; Combinations of machines or engines with driving or driven apparatus (if the apparatus aspects are predominant, see the relevant subclasses for such apparatus, e.g. H02K7/18); Power stations or aggregates (incorporating only machines or engines of positive-displacement type F03C; hydraulic engineering aspects E02B; {combinations with wind energy converters F03D9/008})
Adaptations for drilling wells
Adaptations for use in dentistry {for driving tools or the like having relatively small outer diameter, e.g. pipe cleaning tools}
Stations or aggregates of water-storage type, {e.g. comprising a turbine and a pump}(turbines characterised by having means for functioning alternatively as pumps F03B3/10)
Machine or engine aggregates in dams or the like; Conduits therefor {, e.g. diffusors (bulb groups F03B13/105)}
Submerged units incorporating electric generators or motors
characterised by using wave or tide energy
Controlling (controlling in general G05 {; regulation of plants characterised by the use of siphons F03B13/086})
{Starting, also of pump-turbines}
by varying liquid flow
Other machines or engines

{Installations wherein the liquid circulates in a closed loop (F03B13/06 takes precedence); Alleged perpetua mobilia of this or similar kind (perpetua mobilia using hydrostatic thrust or buoyancy F03B17/04)}
using hydrostatic thrust
using liquid flow {with predominantly kinetic energy conversion}, e.g. of swinging-flap type {, "run-of-river", "ultra-low head" (F03B13/264 takes precedence)}
